title: Acceptance criterion
description: A testable condition that defines when a requirement is satisfied/accepted. Approved acceptance criteria are immutable (supersede, never edit meaning). IDs follow the AC- scheme.

statement.description: The acceptance criterion, phrased testably (Given/When/Then or a clear pass condition).

verification_detail.description: Specifics of how verification is performed (procedure, metric, threshold).

evidence.description: Optional. Evidence backing the criterion at execution time: TEST- ids, commit/PR refs, CI run or golden-sample links. The authoritative live verdict-plus-evidence record is the derived acceptance audit (validation/acceptance-audit.md); this field is a convenience mirror on the criterion itself.

scope.description: Whether the criterion belongs to the MVP or the full target.

source.description: Provenance: the requirement or input that motivated it.
